ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn class exists specifically for the Singaporean ice sweetcorn dessert preparation. FOODON_03315873 (sweet corn food product) is used as the closest defensible anchor. The ice-dessert and evaporated-milk components are not captured by this code.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP covers sweet corn as an ingredient. No FoodEx2 code exists for the composite Singaporean ice sweetcorn dessert with evaporated milk and sugar syrup. This code is used as the closest defensible ingredient-level anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific Singaporean ice dessert preparation. Not assigned to avoid fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of ice sweetcorn is approximately 200 g (one cup), consisting of canned sweetcorn kernels served over crushed ice and drizzled with evaporated milk and sugar syrup.

nutrient_source.title: Ice Sweetcorn — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Ice Sweetcorn (cr_id F02020009). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record with confidence 0.95. Iron was reported as -1 in the source, indicating the value was not available in the HPB record.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Sweet Corn — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for the Singaporean ice sweetcorn dessert preparation. GI value of 52 (low) is derived from sweet corn entries in the University of Sydney GI Database, which report GI values in the range 48–60 for sweet corn. A midpoint value of 52 is applied as the closest defensible analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 15.06) / 100 ≈ 7.8 for a 200 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content at 66 mg per 100 g
hypertension.guidance: Ice sweetcorn is low in sodium (66 mg/100 g), well below the 600 mg/100 g threshold for high-sodium foods; individuals with hypertension may consume a standard 200 g serving without significant blood pressure concern, though added sugar syrup should be minimised and portion size controlled.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate (7.5 g/100 g) with low GI (52) and modest dietary fibre (2.3 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low GI of 52 and moderate carbohydrate content make ice sweetcorn manageable for individuals with type 2 diabetes when consumed in controlled portions (≤200 g); requesting reduced sugar syrup is strongly advised to limit added sugar intake and blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~1.5 g/100 g from evaporated milk) and low sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Ice sweetcorn contains a moderate amount of saturated fat from evaporated milk (approximately 1.5 g/100 g); individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to 150–200 g and request reduced sugar syrup and evaporated milk to keep saturated fat and added sugar within daily targets.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Dietary fibre below 3 g/100 g threshold; no significant anti-inflammatory nutrient profile
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Ice sweetcorn does not meet the anti-inflammatory dietary fibre threshold (2.3 g/100 g vs. 3 g/100 g required) and lacks other meaningful anti-inflammatory factors; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should consume it only occasionally and consider substituting evaporated milk with a lower-saturated-fat alternative.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ice sweetcorn is a popular Singaporean cold dessert or snack consisting of canned sweetcorn kernels served over crushed ice, drizzled with evaporated milk and sweetened with sugar syrup. It is commonly sold at hawker centres, food courts, and dessert stalls across Singapore and is enjoyed year-round as a refreshing treat in the tropical climate. The texture combines the soft, slightly chewy bite of corn kernels with the crunch of ice, and the flavour is mildly sweet with a creamy undertone from the evaporated milk. Nutritionally, ice sweetcorn is relatively low in calories (56 kcal per 100 g) and sodium (66 mg per 100 g), making it one of the lighter dessert options available in Singapore. It provides moderate carbohydrates (7.5 g/100 g), modest dietary fibre (2.3 g/100 g), and a small amount of fat (2.4 g/100 g) primarily from the evaporated milk. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low), which means it produces a relatively gradual rise in blood glucose compared to high-GI desserts. Calcium content is 29 mg per 100 g from the evaporated milk component. The dish is suitable for most dietary patterns including vegetarian and halal diets. Clinically, it is appropriate for individuals managing hypertension due to its low sodium content, and manageable for type 2 diabetes when portion size is controlled and added sugar syrup is reduced. The saturated fat from evaporated milk warrants moderate caution for cardiovascular disease patients.
common_user_queries: Is ice sweetcorn suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F02020009) with a reported confidence of 0.95. The food is a canonical Singaporean hawker dessert with a well-established preparation. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using sweet corn as the closest analogous food, which is a defensible and documented estimation. Multilingual names are verified for Singaporean cuisine requirements.
notes: iron_mg is set to -1: the HPB Singapore Foodo source record (F02020009) reports iron as -1, indicating the value was not available in the HPB database for this specific preparation. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this composite Singaporean ice dessert are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. Saturated fat estimated at 1.52 g/100 g based on the evaporated milk and canned corn composition typical of this preparation, consistent with HPB data for similar ice dessert products; this is an adapted estimate and not directly reported in the source. GI value of 52 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database sweet corn entries (range 48–60); no direct GI measurement exists for the Singaporean ice sweetcorn dessert preparation. The input record described the fat source as coconut milk in the health_suitability section, but the canonical description states evaporated milk; the evaporated milk description is used as it matches the food_name and HPB source record.
